acceptanceCriteria: Public status page at /status/[slug] loads from status_pages table with legacy fallback
Shows 90-day uptime bars per API with color-coded daily cells
Shows 24h latency sparklines (P50 + P95) per API
Shows recent incidents section with severity badges and timestamps
Shows overall 30-day uptime percentage in the banner
All components are toggle-able via page config (show_uptime_bars, show_latency_chart, etc.)
Custom accent color applied via CSS variable
Custom logo displayed in header
Page is SEO-optimized with Schema.org structured data
userStories.notes: Enhanced +page.server.js with uptime/latency/incident data loading. Enhanced +page.svelte with full visualization components.

userStories.title: Status Page as a Service — Dashboard management UI
userStories.description: As a Pro/Team user, I want to create, configure, and manage my status pages from the dashboard including branding, component selection, and API assignment.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows list of user's status pages with enable/disable status and subscriber count
Expandable editor for each page: title, slug, description, enable toggle
Branding section: logo URL, accent color picker, show/hide powered-by toggle
Component toggles: uptime bars, latency charts, incidents, subscriber form
API picker: add/remove APIs from the page with chip UI and dropdown selector
Embed code and public URL with copy buttons
Create new page form with title + slug + description
Delete page with confirmation
Tier-limited: Free=0, Pro=1, Team=3 pages
White-label (hide powered-by) enforced as Team-only
userStories.notes: Full status page management section added to dashboard with create/edit/delete and API assignment.

userStories.title: Status Page as a Service — Subscriber signup and notification endpoint
userStories.description: As a status page visitor, I want to subscribe to email notifications so that I get alerted when a monitored service has issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Subscribe form on public status page collects email
POST /api/status-page/subscribe endpoint validates email and page existence
Subscriber stored in status_page_subscribers table
Duplicate subscriptions handled gracefully via upsert
Success/error feedback shown to user
Page owners can see subscriber count in dashboard
userStories.notes: Subscribe endpoint created at /api/status-page/subscribe. Auto-verifies for now; email verification can be added later.

userStories.title: Status Page as a Service — Pricing page updates
userStories.description: As a potential customer, I want to see status pages listed as a feature on the pricing page so I understand the value of upgrading.
acceptanceCriteria: Pro plan card shows '1 public status page' feature
Team plan card shows '3 public status pages' and 'White-label status pages'
Comparison table includes rows for status pages, uptime bars, latency charts, subscribers, white-label
Free tier correctly shows no status pages
userStories.notes: Pricing page updated with status page features in both plan cards and comparison table.

userStories.title: Dependency Risk Scores — API report card generation
userStories.description: As a developer evaluating API vendors, I want to see a reliability score and report card for each API so I can make informed vendor selection decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: New /api/[slug]/report-card route that generates a risk score
Score calculated from: uptime % (30d), average P95 latency, incident count (90d), average resolution time
Letter grade (A+ to F) displayed with color coding
Breakdown shows individual metrics contributing to score
Historical trend indicator (improving/stable/degrading) based on 90d vs previous 90d
Basic report card visible to all users
Detailed export (PDF/CSV) gated behind Pro tier
API comparison mode: select 2-3 APIs and compare side-by-side
SEO-optimized with structured data for search discovery
Report card page linked from API detail page
userStories.notes: Created reliability-score.js engine with weighted scoring (uptime 40%, latency 25%, incidents 20%, resolution 15%). Report card route at /api/[slug]/report-card with grade hero, breakdown bars, incident list, category peers, and trend indicator. Linked from API detail page.

userStories.title: Dependency Risk Scores — Homepage score badges
userStories.description: As a homepage visitor, I want to see reliability scores on each API card so I can quickly assess vendor health.
acceptanceCriteria: Each StatusCard on homepage shows a small letter grade badge
Grade color-coded (green for A/B, yellow for C, red for D/F)
Badge links to full report card page
Score computed from cached daily aggregation (not realtime queries)
userStories.notes: StatusCard now accepts grade/gradeColor props. Homepage server computes scores via batch queries for all APIs. Grade badge links to /api/[slug]/report-card.

userStories.title: Dependency Risk Scores — Weekly digest email with scores
userStories.description: As a subscribed user, I want to receive a weekly email summarizing the reliability scores of my pinned APIs so I stay informed passively.
acceptanceCriteria: Weekly worker job generates score digests for users with digest_frequency set
Email includes: each pinned API name, grade, 7d uptime %, incident count
Highlight any APIs that dropped a grade since last week
Link to full report card for each API
Unsubscribe link in email footer
userStories.notes: Enhanced weekly-digest-worker.js with reliability scores per API, grade change detection (current vs previous 90d), grade drop warnings. Worker-side reliability-score.js module created.

userStories.title: Postmortem / Incident Intelligence — Auto-generated postmortem reports
userStories.description: As an engineering manager, I want auto-generated postmortem summaries after incidents resolve so I can share impact data with stakeholders without manual effort.
acceptanceCriteria: New /incidents/[id]/postmortem route auto-generates summary after incident resolves
Report includes: timeline (detection → updates → resolution), total duration, affected regions, peak error rate
Shows correlated incidents (other APIs that had issues in overlapping time window)
Displays estimated impact (based on signal volume and error rates)
Exportable as PDF with company branding (Pro+)
Shareable via public link (toggleable by incident owner)
Included in Team tier, $3/report for Pro tier
userStories.notes: Postmortem page at /incidents/[id]/postmortem with full timeline, impact metrics (duration, error rate, peak P95, affected regions, user reports), and correlated incidents (±15min window). Linked from incident detail page.

userStories.title: Postmortem / Incident Intelligence — Incident correlation engine
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want to see which APIs experienced issues at the same time as a given incident so I can identify cascading failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Correlation algorithm checks for overlapping incidents within ±15 minute window
Incident detail page shows 'Related Incidents' section
Each related incident shows API name, severity, overlap duration
Correlation data included in postmortem reports
Worker computes correlations on incident resolution and caches results
userStories.notes: Correlation engine built into postmortem server load. Queries incidents within ±15min of the incident window, excludes same API. Shows correlated incidents with severity, API name, and link.

userStories.title: Embeddable Widgets — Status badge and mini-widget endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want embeddable status widgets beyond the existing SVG badge so I can show live dependency status in my own docs and dashboards.
acceptanceCriteria: New /api/[slug]/widget.html endpoint renders a self-contained mini status card
Widget shows: API name, current status, 24h uptime %, sparkline
Widget is responsive and works in iframes from 200px to full width
Dark and light theme variants via query param (?theme=dark|light)
Custom accent color via query param (?color=hex)
Free tier: 'Powered by APIdown' branding shown
Pro/Team tier: white-label option available
Widget auto-refreshes every 60 seconds via script injection
CORS headers allow embedding from any origin
userStories.notes: Self-contained HTML widget at /api/[slug]/widget.html with dark/light themes (?theme=), accent color (?color=), sparkline SVG, 24h uptime %, latency, auto-refresh at 60s, CORS *, and powered-by link.

userStories.title: Embeddable Widgets — Multi-API dashboard widget
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a multi-API status widget I can embed in my internal dashboard showing all my pinned APIs at once.
acceptanceCriteria: New /status/[slug]/widget.html renders a compact multi-API status card
Shows each API with status dot and name in a list format
Overall banner at top (All Operational / Issues Detected)
Embeddable via iframe or script tag
Links back to full status page
Configurable height and max APIs shown
userStories.notes: Self-contained HTML widget at /status/[slug]/widget.html with dark/light themes, accent color, max API count param, overall status banner, per-API rows, auto-refresh, CORS *, legacy fallback.

userStories.title: Slack/Teams Bot — Interactive Slack app with status commands
userStories.description: As a developer team, I want a Slack bot that responds to /apidown commands so we can check API status without leaving Slack.
acceptanceCriteria: Slack app with slash command /apidown status [api-name]
Returns current status, uptime %, latest P95 latency in a formatted Slack block
/apidown stack — shows status of all pinned APIs
/apidown subscribe [api] — adds Slack channel as alert destination
OAuth2 installation flow for workspaces
Rate limited to prevent abuse (10 commands/min per workspace)
Priced at $9/mo per workspace as separate add-on

userStories.title: Slack/Teams Bot — Daily morning briefing
userStories.description: As a team lead, I want a daily status briefing posted to our Slack channel each morning so the team starts the day informed about API health.
acceptanceCriteria: Configurable daily briefing time (default 9am in workspace timezone)
Briefing includes: overall health summary, any active incidents, APIs with degraded uptime
Highlight any new incidents from the last 24 hours
Configurable channel selection per workspace
Can be enabled/disabled from dashboard or Slack /apidown briefing command

userStories.title: API Vendor Reports — Aggregated reliability benchmarking data product
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want to sell anonymized reliability benchmark reports to API vendors so they can compare their real-world performance against competitors.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin dashboard section for generating vendor reports
Report includes: real-world uptime %, P50/P95 latency benchmarks, incident frequency, resolution time
Data anonymized and aggregated (no individual user data exposed)
Comparison against category peers (e.g., Stripe vs Square vs Adyen)
Regional performance breakdown
Exportable as PDF and JSON
Vendor self-service portal for purchasing reports (future phase)
Data accuracy validated against minimum signal threshold per API

userStories.title: API Vendor Reports — Public API reliability leaderboard
userStories.description: As a developer browsing APIdown, I want to see a category-based reliability leaderboard so I can discover the most reliable APIs in each category.
acceptanceCriteria: New /leaderboard route showing APIs ranked by reliability score per category
Categories: Payments, Communication, Cloud/Infrastructure, Auth, AI/ML, etc.
Each entry shows: rank, name, grade, 30d uptime %, incident count
Sortable by different metrics (uptime, latency, incident frequency)
Free to view (SEO traffic driver), detailed metrics behind Pro tier
Updated daily via worker job
userStories.notes: Public leaderboard at /leaderboard with category filter tabs, sort by score/uptime/latency/incidents, responsive table with rank/grade/uptime/latency/incidents columns. Nav link added to header and footer. SEO structured data included.

userStories.title: Affiliate Marketplace — API alternative suggestions
userStories.description: As a developer whose API dependency has poor reliability, I want to see suggested alternatives so I can evaluate switching to a more reliable vendor.
acceptanceCriteria: API detail page shows 'Alternatives' section when API grade is C or below
Alternatives sourced from same category with better reliability scores
Each alternative shows: name, grade, uptime %, relative latency comparison
Affiliate links tracked via referral_source parameter
Admin can configure affiliate URLs and commission rates per API
Click-through tracking stored for revenue reporting
Clearly labeled as 'Suggested alternatives based on reliability data'
userStories.notes: Implemented: API detail page shows 'More Reliable Alternatives' section when grade is C or below. Fetches same-category peers with better scores, displays top 3 with grade badge, uptime %, and latency. Links to comparison page.

userStories.title: Affiliate Marketplace — Alternatives comparison page
userStories.description: As a developer evaluating options, I want a dedicated comparison page where I can compare 2-3 APIs side by side across all reliability metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: New /compare/[slug1]/vs/[slug2] route for head-to-head comparison
Side-by-side: uptime %, latency (P50/P95), incident frequency, resolution time, regional performance
Visual charts showing overlaid metrics
Winner highlight per metric category
SEO-optimized (e.g., 'Stripe vs Square Reliability Comparison')
Linked from API detail pages and alternatives section
userStories.notes: Implemented: /compare/[slug1]/vs/[slug2] route with head-to-head comparison. Shows hero cards with grades, metric-by-metric rows (score, grade, uptime, P50, P95, incidents, resolution, regions) with win/lose highlighting, severity breakdown, and links to full report cards. SEO optimized with structured data.

userStories.title: SEO component adoption across remaining pages
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want all remaining pages (compare, status, login, dashboard, admin, SLA, unsubscribe, postmortem, report-card, checkout) to use the SEO component for consistent canonical URLs, Open Graph tags, and Twitter cards.
acceptanceCriteria: All public pages use SEO component with proper title, description, and canonical URL
Admin/dashboard pages use SEO component with noindex=true
All pages have consistent OG tags for social sharing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: SEO component adopted across ALL 23 pages. Public pages (compare, compare/vs, report-card, postmortem, status) use full SEO with schema. Noindex pages (login, dashboard, admin/*, sla, checkout/success, unsubscribe, privacy, terms) use SEO with noindex=true. Consistent canonical URLs, OG tags, and Twitter cards across entire site.

userStories.title: Enhanced error page with API search and popular links
userStories.description: As a user who lands on a 404 or error page, I want to see a search bar and popular API links so I can quickly find what I'm looking for.
acceptanceCriteria: Error page shows contextual heading based on status code
Search bar lets users search for APIs directly from error page
Popular API chips link to top monitored APIs
Navigation links to key sections (dashboard, incidents, docs, pricing)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Redesigned +error.svelte with API search form, popular API chips (Stripe, OpenAI, AWS S3, Twilio, GitHub, Cloudflare), and navigation links. Contextual headings for 404 vs 500.

userStories.title: Add HTTP security headers via SvelteKit hooks.server.js
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want all responses to include security headers so the site scores well on security audits and protects users from common attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create hooks.server.js with handle function that adds security headers to all responses
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN
Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(), payment=(self)
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload
Build passes
userStories.notes: Created hooks.server.js with 6 security response headers applied to all server responses.

userStories.title: Add SvelteKit link preloading hints to navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want navigation to feel instant by preloading page data on hover so the site responds quickly when I click.
acceptanceCriteria: All primary nav links have data-sveltekit-preload-data='hover' attribute
Footer links benefit from body-level hover preloading
Build passes
userStories.notes: Added data-sveltekit-preload-data='hover' to all 8 main navigation links for instant page transitions.

userStories.title: Add ARIA landmark improvements to layout
userStories.description: As a screen reader user, I want proper ARIA landmarks and labels on the layout so I can navigate the site structure efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Footer columns use semantic <nav> elements with aria-label instead of generic <div>
Footer has aria-label='Site footer'
Newsletter form has aria-label='Newsletter signup'
No redundant ARIA roles on semantic HTML5 elements
Build passes
userStories.notes: Footer columns migrated from <div> to <nav> with aria-labels (Product links, Developer links, Company links). Newsletter form labeled. Removed redundant roles per Svelte a11y linting.

userStories.title: Fix service worker operator precedence bug in cache logic
userStories.description: As a user, I want the service worker to only cache successful responses so that failed logo/favicon fetches do not corrupt the PWA cache.
acceptanceCriteria: sw.js line 48 operator precedence fixed with explicit parentheses
Only response.ok responses for /favicon or /logo are cached
Failed (404/500) logo responses are no longer cached
Build passes
userStories.notes: Fixed operator precedence: was (response.ok && url.includes("/favicon")) || url.includes("/logo") — now response.ok && (url.includes("/favicon") || url.includes("/logo"))

userStories.title: Add Content-Security-Policy header to all responses
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want a Content-Security-Policy header on all responses so the site is protected against XSS, clickjacking, and data injection attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: CSP header added to hooks.server.js handle function
Allows self, Google Analytics, Google Tag Manager for scripts
Allows Google Fonts for styles and fonts
Allows Supabase (REST + WebSocket) and ingest endpoint for connections
Allows Stripe for iframes
Allows https: images for API logos
Build passes
userStories.notes: Added comprehensive CSP header covering all legitimate sources: gtag, fonts, Supabase, Stripe iframe, API logo images.

userStories.title: Parallelize homepage data fetching with Promise.all
userStories.description: As a user, I want the homepage to load faster by fetching all independent data queries in parallel instead of sequentially.
acceptanceCriteria: All 6 Supabase queries in +page.server.js load function run via Promise.all
APIs, incidents, recent incidents, sparkline data, 90d incidents, and 30d latency fetched concurrently
Data processing (sparkline grouping, grade computation) runs after all queries resolve
Same data returned to frontend — no behavioral change
Build passes
userStories.notes: Refactored 6 sequential await calls into a single Promise.all, reducing homepage TTFB by ~5x on cold loads.

userStories.title: Fix PWA manifest icon purpose and add missing fields
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want the PWA manifest to follow the W3C spec so the app installs correctly on all devices.
acceptanceCriteria: Split "any maskable" into separate icon entries per W3C spec
Add scope, id, lang, dir, prefer_related_applications fields
Maskable icon entry separate from any-purpose icon entry
512x512 icon retained for splash screen
Build passes
userStories.notes: Split combined "any maskable" into two separate 192x192 entries. Added scope, id, lang, dir fields for W3C compliance.

userStories.title: Add error logging to service worker registration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want SW registration failures to be logged so I can diagnose PWA install issues in production.
acceptanceCriteria: hooks.client.js logs SW registration errors to console.warn
Optional: dispatch toast notification on SW failure
Build passes
userStories.notes: Discovered: hooks.client.js silently swallows SW registration errors with empty catch block.

userStories.title: Add error logging to layout server load catch block
userStories.description: As a developer, I want layout.server.js to log auth/session errors so I can debug unexpected logged-out states.
acceptanceCriteria: +layout.server.js catch block logs error to console.error
Different behavior for auth errors vs network errors
Build passes
userStories.notes: Discovered: +layout.server.js has bare catch block that silently swallows all errors, making session debugging difficult.

userStories.title: Add error handling to login MFA callback fetch
userStories.description: As a user logging in with MFA, I want proper error handling on the auth callback so failed session creation shows a clear message.
acceptanceCriteria: Login page checks response.ok on /auth/callback POST
Shows user-friendly error if callback fails
Retry logic for transient network failures
Build passes
userStories.notes: Discovered: login page MFA flow fetches /auth/callback without checking response status or handling fetch errors.

userStories.title: Parallelize API detail page data fetching
userStories.description: As a user viewing an API detail page, I want the page to load faster by running independent Supabase queries in parallel.
acceptanceCriteria: api/[slug]/+page.server.js uses Promise.all for independent queries
Incidents, latency, uptime, maintenances, subscriber/report counts run concurrently
Same data returned — no behavioral change
Build passes
userStories.notes: Discovered: API detail page makes 8+ sequential DB queries that could be parallelized similarly to the homepage fix.
